originalTitle: Wheat Field with Cypresses — Vincent van Gogh

A1.title: A field of gold
A1.text: This is a painting. The painter is Vincent van Gogh. He is from the Netherlands. The painting shows a field. The wheat is yellow like gold. The wind moves the wheat. On the right stands a dark tree. It is a cypress. The sky is blue and white. The clouds turn like waves. There are mountains behind the field. Van Gogh paints with thick color. The picture feels alive. Today it hangs in a museum in New York.

vocabulary.definition: a yellow plant; we make bread from it
a white or grey thing in the sky

A2.title: A summer field by Van Gogh
A2.text: Vincent van Gogh painted this picture in 1889. He was in the south of France then. He lived in a hospital because he was ill. From the hills he saw this landscape many times. The painting shows a golden wheat field in summer. The wind blows through the wheat. On the right, a tall dark cypress stands like a green flame. Behind the field there are blue mountains and olive trees. The sky is full of white clouds. They turn and roll like waves in the sea. Van Gogh used thick paint and strong colors. He loved this picture very much. Today it is in the Metropolitan Museum in New York.

vocabulary.definition: a picture of nature: fields, trees, sky
a small tree; its fruit gives oil
not thin; with a lot of material

B1.title: Wheat Field with Cypresses: a landscape in motion
B1.text: Wheat Field with Cypresses is one of Vincent van Gogh's most famous landscapes. He painted it in June 1889, while he was staying in a hospital in Saint-Rémy, in the south of France. From the hills near the hospital he could see golden fields, olive trees and the dark cypresses of Provence. In the painting, everything seems to move. The wheat bends in the wind, the olive trees twist, and the clouds roll across the sky like white waves. The tall cypress on the right rises like a green flame and connects the earth with the sky. Van Gogh put the paint on the canvas in short, thick strokes, so the surface itself feels alive. He wrote to his brother that he considered this one of his best summer paintings, and he even made several versions of it. Today visitors can see this one at the Metropolitan Museum of Art in New York.

vocabulary.definition: the strong cloth that painters paint on
one movement of the painter's brush
to move into a curved shape
the top or outside part of something
to think of something in a certain way

B2.title: Van Gogh's restless summer landscape
B2.text: Painted in June 1889, Wheat Field with Cypresses belongs to the most productive and dramatic period of Vincent van Gogh's short career. The artist had entered the asylum of Saint-Paul-de-Mausole in Saint-Rémy the month before, and the view from the surrounding hills — ripe wheat, wind-bent olive trees and the flame-like cypresses of Provence — became one of his central motifs. What makes the picture unforgettable is its movement. Nothing in it stands still: the golden field sways, the olives twist, and the clouds curl across the sky in thick white spirals that seem almost solid. The dark cypress at the right acts as a vertical anchor, linking the warm earth to the cool, restless sky. Van Gogh applied the oil paint in short, loaded brushstrokes, building a surface with a texture you can almost feel, and the colors follow his favorite contrast: golden yellow against deep blue and green. He was proud of the result and told his brother Theo that he regarded it as one of his best summer landscapes; he even painted several versions of the composition. The one that hangs in the Metropolitan Museum of Art in New York is the most celebrated.

vocabulary.definition: a hospital where people with mental illness were cared for
a subject or image an artist returns to again and again
to move slowly from side to side
something that holds a thing firmly in place
a single mark made with a painter's brush
the way a surface feels or looks

C1.title: Wheat, wind and flame: Van Gogh at Saint-Rémy
C1.text: Few paintings communicate the nervous energy of Vincent van Gogh's late style as vividly as Wheat Field with Cypresses, painted in June 1889 during his stay at the asylum of Saint-Paul-de-Mausole in Saint-Rémy-de-Provence. Barely a month after the crises in Arles had forced him to seek treatment, he was working outdoors again, and the wheat fields below the Alpilles hills offered him a subject in which observation and emotion could merge. The composition is deceptively simple — a horizontal band of ripening wheat, a screen of olive trees, distant blue mountains and an immense sky — yet every element has been set in motion. The wheat leans in golden waves; the olives writhe; the clouds coil upon themselves like cream stirred into coffee. Against all this horizontal turbulence, the great cypress rises at the right edge like a dark flame, binding earth and sky together. Van Gogh's brushwork does much of the expressive work: the paint is laid on thickly, stroke by visible stroke, so that the surface ripples with the same agitation as the landscape it depicts. He knew he had succeeded. To his brother Theo he described the picture as one of his best summer canvases, and he repeated the composition in further versions. The one now at the Metropolitan Museum of Art in New York has become a defining image of his year in Provence.

vocabulary.definition: in a way that gives a false impression of being simple
to twist the body as if in pain
to wind around in circles or spirals
to show or represent in a picture

C2.title: The quintessential Van Gogh
C2.text: There is a case to be made that Wheat Field with Cypresses is the quintessential Van Gogh — the picture in which his conviction that a landscape could carry the full weight of human feeling comes off without a hitch. He painted it in June 1889 at the asylum of Saint-Paul-de-Mausole in Saint-Rémy, where he had committed himself after the crises of Arles. Permitted to work in the fields below the Alpilles, he returned obsessively to a handful of motifs — the olive groves, the walled wheat field and, above all, the cypresses, which he felt had never yet been painted as he saw them, comparing their line and proportion to an Egyptian obelisk. On this canvas the whole of Provence appears to be in ferment. The wheat runs molten gold; the olives buck and heave like a green surf; and overhead the clouds curdle into those unmistakable spirals that no reproduction ever quite tames. Against the commotion the cypress stands like a plume of dark fire — the one emphatic vertical in a symphony of horizontals, stitching the baked earth to a cold, churning sky. The paint itself supplies the drama: laid on wet and thick, ridge upon ridge, it turns the canvas into something close to a low relief, so that the picture's agitation is literal as well as depicted. Van Gogh judged it one of his best summer landscapes — a rare flash of confidence from a painter who sold almost nothing in his lifetime — and posterity, filing past it daily at the Metropolitan Museum of Art, has seen no reason to overrule him.

vocabulary.definition: representing the purest, most perfect example of something
smoothly, with no problems at all
in a state of restless excitement or turmoil
to thicken into clots, like spoiled milk
sculpture that projects only slightly from a flat surface

A1.title: Buğday Tarlası ve Sedirler
A1.text: Buğday tarlası var. Renkler sarı ve mavi. Gök güzel. Ağaçlar yeşil. Tabloda hareket var. Rüzgar var. Van Gogh bu resmi yaptı.

A2.title: Buğday Tarlası ve Sedirler
A2.text: Vincent van Gogh, 1889'da bu resmi yaptı. Resimde bir buğday tarlası var. Tarlada altın sarısı buğday görüyoruz. Arka planda yeşil zeytin ağaçları var. Gökyüzü mavi ve bulutlar beyaz. Resim çok hareketli. Van Gogh, renkleri çok canlı kullandı. Koyu bir sedir ağacı sağda duruyor. Tüm tablo güzel bir yaz manzarası sunuyor.

B1.title: Van Gogh'un Buğday Tarlası ve Sedirler Tablosu
B1.text: Vincent van Gogh, 1889 yılında bu tabloda bir yaz manzarası çizmiştir. Tabloda altın sarısı buğday tarlası öne çıkıyor. Tarlanın arkasında yeşil zeytin ağaçları ve koyu bir sedir ağacı yer alıyor. Gökyüzü, kalın beyaz bulutlarla birlikte mavi tonlarda. Van Gogh'un kullandığı renkler oldukça canlı ve etkileyici. Altın sarısı buğday, derin mavi ve yeşil tonları ile mükemmel bir kontrast oluşturuyor. Tabloda her şey hareket ediyor gibi görünüyor. Van Gogh, bu resmi çok sevmiştir ve kardeşi Theo'ya bunun en iyi yaz manzaralarından biri olduğunu yazmıştır.

B2.title: Bir Van Gogh Eseri: Buğday Tarlası ve Sedirler
B2.text: Vincent van Gogh, Haziran 1889'da 'Buğday Tarlası ve Sedirler' adlı eserini yaratmıştır. Tabloda, görkemli bir yaz manzarası gözler önüne serilmektedir. Altın sarısı buğday tarlası, derin mavi gökyüzü ve yeşil zeytin ağaçları arasında dikkat çekmektedir. Buğday, rüzgarla dans ederken, ağaçlar da hareket ediyor gibi görünür. Van Gogh, tabloyu kısa ve yoğun fırça darbeleriyle betimlemiştir, bu da esere dokunsal bir derinlik kazandırır. Çizimdeki karanlık sedir ağacı sağda durarak, sıcak toprak ve serin gökyüzü arasında bir bağlantı kurar. Bu eser, Van Gogh'un yaz manzaraları arasında en çok beğenilendir ve sanatçının kardeşi Theo'ya göre de en iyi yaz manzaralarındandır.

C1.title: Van Gogh'un 'Buğday Tarlası ve Sedirler' Eseri Üzerine
C1.text: Vincent van Gogh'un 1889'daki 'Buğday Tarlası ve Sedirler' eseri, sanatçının en üretken dönemlerinden birine aittir. Tablo, Saint-Rémy'deki Saint-Paul-de-Mausole akıl hastanesinde kaldığı süre zarfında yapılmıştır. Van Gogh, çevresindeki zeytin ağaçları ve Provence bölgesinin ateş gibi yükselen sedirleri gibi natüralist unsurları kullanarak bu eseri oluşturmuştur. Altın sarısı buğday tarlası, renk zıtlığı ve yoğun hareket ile adeta canlanmaktadır. Koyu sedir ağacı, kompozisyonda dikey bir denge unsuru olarak görünmektedir. Kıvrılan bulutlar ve dalgalanan ağaçlar, izleyiciye canlı bir yaz manzarası sunar. Van Gogh, bu çalışması için kardeşi Theo'ya, bunun yaz manzaralarındaki en iyi eserlerinden biri olduğunu ifade etmiştir. Eseri gündelik yaşamın bir parçası gibi göstermesi, Van Gogh'un güçlü duygularını ve doğa ile olan derin bağını yansıtır.

vocabulary.definition: Çok sayıda ve kaliteli üretim yapan.

C2.title: Van Gogh'un 'Buğday Tarlası ve Sedirler' Eserinin Estetik Değeri
C2.text: Vincent van Gogh'un 'Buğday Tarlası ve Sedirler' adlı eseri, 1889 yılında sanatçının Saint-Rémy'deki akıl hastanesinde geçirdiği süre zarfında ortaya çıkmıştır. Bu eser, Van Gogh'un içinde bulunduğu ruhsal karmaşayı ve doğa ile kurduğu derin bağı fantastik bir şekilde yansıtmaktadır. Altın sarısı buğday tarlası, gökyüzünün derin mavi ve yeşil tonlarıyla oluşturduğu çarpıcı zıtlık ile dikkati çekiyor. Van Gogh, kısa ve yoğun fırça darbeleriyle, esere hareket ve yaşam katmıştır. Sağdaki karanlık sedir ağacı, bir denge unsuru işlevi görerek, sıcak toprak ile serin gökyüzü arasında bir köprü kurmaktadır. Kıvrılan ve dans eden bulutlar, tablonun dinamizmini artırmakta ve izleyiciye adeta yazın canlı bir portresini sunmaktadır. Van Gogh, bu eseri için ‘en iyi yaz manzaralarından biri’ demiştir. Resmin estetik değeri, döneminin sıradan yaşamını da gözler önüne sererek, izleyiciyi hem düşündürmekte hem de duygulandırmaktadır. Sanatçının güçlü duygusu, resmin içine sinmiş durumda ve bu da esere derin bir duygusal etki katmaktadır.
